heading: WTC Health Program Scientific/Technical Advisory Committee; WTC Health Program Steering Committees
label: 300mm–1 WTC Health Program Scientific/Technical Advisory Committee; WTC Health Program Steering Committees
notes: Amendments2022—Subsec. (a)(6). substituted “chapter 10 of title 5” for “FACA” in heading and “Chapter 10 of title 5.” for “the Federal Advisory Committee Act.” in text.Pub. L. 117–286
amendments: Amendments2022—Subsec. (a)(6). substituted “chapter 10 of title 5” for “FACA” in heading and “Chapter 10 of title 5.” for “the Federal Advisory Committee Act.” in text.Pub. L. 117–286

children.heading: Establishment
children.label: 1 Establishment
children.text: The WTC Program Administrator shall establish an advisory committee to be known as the WTC Health Program Scientific/Technical Advisory Committee (in this subsection referred to as the “Advisory Committee”) to review scientific and medical evidence and to make recommendations to the Administrator on additional WTC Program eligibility criteria and on additional WTC-related health conditions.

children.label: A
children.text: 4 occupational physicians, at least 2 of whom have experience treating WTC rescue and recovery workers;

children.label: B
children.text: 1 physician with expertise in pulmonary medicine;

children.label: C
children.text: 2 environmental medicine or environmental health specialists;

children.label: D
children.text: 2 representatives of WTC responders;

children.label: E
children.text: 2 representatives of certified-eligible WTC survivors;

children.label: F
children.text: an industrial hygienist;

children.label: G
children.text: a toxicologist;

children.label: H
children.text: an epidemiologist; and

children.label: I
children.text: a mental health professional.

children.heading: Meetings
children.label: 3 Meetings
children.text: The Advisory Committee shall meet at such frequency as may be required to carry out its duties.

children.heading: Reports
children.label: 4 Reports
children.text: The WTC Program Administrator shall provide for publication of recommendations of the Advisory Committee on the public Web site established for the WTC Program.

children.heading: Duration
children.label: 5 Duration
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, the Advisory Committee shall continue in operation during the period in which the WTC Program is in operation.

children.heading: Application of chapter 10 of title 5
children.label: 6 Application of chapter 10 of title 5
children.text: Except as otherwise specifically provided, the Advisory Committee shall be subject to Chapter 10 of title 5.1So in original. Probably should not be capitalized.1

children.heading: WTC Health Program Steering Committees
children.label: b WTC Health Program Steering Committees

children.heading: WTC Responders Steering Committee
children.label: A WTC Responders Steering Committee
children.text: One Steering Committee, to be known as the WTC Responders Steering Committee, for the purpose of receiving input from affected stakeholders and facilitating the coordination of monitoring and treatment programs for the enrolled WTC responders under subpart 1 of part B.

children.heading: WTC Survivors Steering Committee
children.label: B WTC Survivors Steering Committee
children.text: One Steering Committee, to be known as the WTC Survivors Steering Committee, for the purpose of receiving input from affected stakeholders and facilitating the coordination of initial health evaluations, monitoring, and treatment programs for screening-eligible and certified-eligible WTC survivors under subpart 2 of part B.

children.label: I
children.text: representatives of the Centers of Excellence providing services to WTC responders;

children.label: II
children.text: representatives of labor organizations representing firefighters, police, other New York City employees, and recovery and cleanup workers who responded to the , terrorist attacks; andSeptember 11, 2001

children.label: III
children.text: 3 representatives of New York City, 1 of whom will be selected by the police commissioner of New York City, 1 by the health commissioner of New York City, and 1 by the mayor of New York City.

children.heading: Initial membership
children.label: ii Initial membership
children.text: The WTC Responders Steering Committee shall initially be composed of members of the WTC Monitoring and Treatment Program Steering Committee (as in existence on the day before ).January 2, 2011

children.label: I
children.text: the Centers of Excellence providing services to screening-eligible and certified-eligible WTC survivors;

children.label: II
children.text: the population of residents, students, and area and other workers affected by the , terrorist attacks;September 11, 2001

children.label: III
children.text: screening-eligible and certified-eligible survivors receiving initial health evaluations, monitoring, or treatment under subpart 2 of part B and organizations advocating on their behalf; and

children.label: IV
children.text: New York City.

children.heading: Initial membership
children.label: ii Initial membership
children.text: The WTC Survivors Steering Committee shall initially be composed of members of the WTC Environmental Health Center Survivor Advisory Committee (as in existence on the day before ).January 2, 2011

children.heading: Additional appointments
children.label: C Additional appointments
children.text: Each Steering Committee may recommend, if approved by a majority of voting members of the Committee, additional members to the Committee.

children.heading: Vacancies
children.label: D Vacancies
children.text: A vacancy in a Steering Committee shall be filled by an individual recommended by the Steering Committee.
